_comment: Deterministic mock LLM responses for enrichment tests (Phase 5.1). Keys are SHA-256 hex digests of the serialized prompt messages (same key used by MockChatProvider). Values are JSON strings the LLM would return.

default.detailed_summary: This section presents the core methodology of the paper. The authors describe a novel retrieval-augmented approach that combines dense and sparse retrieval signals. Experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate significant improvements over prior baselines. The method achieves state-of-the-art results while remaining computationally efficient.
default.compact_summary: Novel RAG approach combining dense and sparse retrieval achieves state-of-the-art results.

definitions.definition: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, a technique that grounds language model outputs in retrieved documents

claims.statement: We demonstrate that our method outperforms all baselines on the TREC benchmark.

methods.name: Hybrid Retrieval
methods.summary: Combines BM25 sparse retrieval with dense vector search using reciprocal rank fusion.

limitations.statement: However, the approach requires significant GPU memory for the dense retrieval component.

document_default.overview: This paper presents Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation (AdaRAG), a novel framework that dynamically adjusts retrieval strategies based on query complexity. The authors propose a multi-stage pipeline combining BM25 sparse retrieval with dense vector search and a learned query router. Experiments on five scientific QA benchmarks demonstrate that AdaRAG consistently outperforms static RAG baselines by 4-8% on F1. The method introduces a lightweight complexity classifier that routes queries to the most appropriate retrieval strategy. Ablation studies confirm that each component of the pipeline contributes independently to overall performance gains.

contributions.title: Adaptive Query Router
contributions.summary: A lightweight neural classifier that routes queries to BM25, dense, or hybrid retrieval based on estimated query complexity.

contributions.title: Multi-Stage Reranking
contributions.summary: A two-stage reranking pipeline using cross-encoder scores followed by diversity-aware selection improves final answer quality.

methods.name: BM25 Sparse Retrieval
methods.description: Traditional term-frequency based retrieval using BM25 scoring over the document corpus.

methods.name: Dense Vector Search
methods.description: Approximate nearest neighbour search over BERT-encoded document embeddings using HNSW indexing.

findings.statement: AdaRAG achieves +6.3% F1 on the SciQ benchmark compared to the best static baseline.
The query router correctly classifies 87% of queries into the optimal retrieval strategy.

limitations.text: The complexity classifier requires labelled query-difficulty annotations which may be expensive to obtain for new domains.

conclusions.statement: Future work should explore reinforcement-learning based routing policies that adapt online to user feedback.
AdaRAG demonstrates that adaptive retrieval strategy selection is a more effective approach than a single fixed retrieval method.
